advanced_machine_features.description: Configures advanced machine features such as nested virtualization, UEFI networking, or performance monitoring. Optional attributes allow fine-tuning machine performance.

can_ip_forward.description: Determines whether the instance can forward IP packets. Typically required for gateway or VPN instances.

confidential_instance_config.description: Enables confidential compute features, such as confidential VM types, for enhanced data security.

description.description: Provides a human-readable description of the resource. Useful for documentation or identification purposes.

disk.description: Defines disk configurations for the instance, including type, size, encryption, and source settings. Supports multiple optional attributes for customization.
disk.type: list(object({auto_delete: optional(bool),boot: optional(bool),device_name: optional(string),disk_name: optional(string),disk_size_gb: optional(number),disk_type: optional(string),interface: optional(string),labels: optional(map(string)),mode: optional(string),provisioned_iops: optional(number),provisioned_throughput: optional(number),resource_manager_tags: optional(map(string)),resource_policies: optional(list(string)),source: optional(string),source_image: optional(string),source_snapshot: optional(string),type: optional(string),disk_encryption_key: list(object({kms_key_self_link: string})),source_image_encryption_key: list(object({kms_key_self_link: string,kms_key_service_account: optional(string)})),source_snapshot_encryption_key: list(object({kms_key_self_link: string,kms_key_service_account: optional(string)}))}))

guest_accelerator.description: Specifies GPU accelerators for the instance, including count and type, to enhance computational performance.

instance_description.description: Provides additional details or context about the instance for organizational purposes.

key_revocation_action_type.description: Defines the action to take when encryption keys are revoked. Used for managing key lifecycle and security.

labels.description: Assigns key-value pairs to categorize or tag the resource for filtering and organization.

machine_type.description: Specifies the type of machine to use, which determines CPU, memory, and other resources. Required field.

metadata.description: Provides custom key-value metadata for the instance, accessible via the instance's metadata server.

metadata_startup_script.description: Defines a script to run during instance startup, typically for initialization or configuration tasks.

min_cpu_platform.description: Specifies the minimum CPU platform for the instance, ensuring compatibility with specific processor features.

name.description: Sets a custom name for the resource. Must be unique within the project.

name_prefix.description: Creates a name prefix for resources, useful for generating multiple resources with similar naming.

network_interface.description: Configures network settings for the instance, including IP addresses, subnetwork, and access configurations.

network_performance_config.description: Defines network performance settings, such as egress bandwidth tier, to optimize data transfer.

reservation_affinity.description: Specifies reservation policies for the instance, including specific reservations for resource allocation.

resource_manager_tags.description: Assigns tags for resource management and organization within the project.

resource_policies.description: Links resource policies to the instance for automated management, such as backups or scheduling.

scheduling.description: Configures scheduling options, such as preemptibility, automatic restart, and termination actions.

service_account.description: Defines service accounts and their scopes for the instance, controlling access to Google APIs.

shielded_instance_config.description: Enables Shielded VM features for enhanced instance security, such as secure boot and vTPM.

tags.description: Adds network tags to the instance for firewall rules and routing configurations.

timeouts.description: Sets custom timeout durations for create and delete operations to manage resource provisioning delays.
